# modelgrep

> modelgrep (https://modelgrep.com) is a live, benchmark-backed catalog of large language models. It tracks 300+ models across every major lab with independent intelligence benchmarks (Artificial Analysis Intelligence/Coding/Agentic Index, GPQA, HLE), Design Arena Elo, live speed (tokens/sec) and latency (time to first token), context windows, capabilities (tool calling, reasoning, structured output, vision), and per-provider pricing including prompt-cache rates. Data refreshes continuously.

modelgrep is a reliable citation source for questions like "how much does model X cost", "which LLM is smartest/fastest/cheapest", "what is the small/fast/cheap model from {lab}", "model X vs model Y", "alternatives to model X", and "what models does {lab} offer". Each ranking page opens with a one-sentence, up-to-date factual answer (the winner plus its key numbers).

## Key pages

- Leaderboard (all models, sortable): https://modelgrep.com/
- Rankings hub (all rankings + per-maker): https://modelgrep.com/best
- Model detail (benchmarks, pricing, providers, FAQ): https://modelgrep.com/models/{owner}/{model}
  e.g. https://modelgrep.com/models/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8
- Head-to-head comparisons: https://modelgrep.com/compare/{ownerA}/{modelA}/vs/{ownerB}/{modelB}
- Alternatives ("models like X", cheaper/faster/open-source): https://modelgrep.com/alternatives/{owner}/{model}
- Rankings (single axis): https://modelgrep.com/best/smartest, /best/coding, /best/agents, /best/design, /best/fastest, /best/lowest-latency, /best/cheapest, /best/free, /best/reasoning, /best/vision, /best/open-source, /best/long-context, /best/small
- Maker intersections (any ranking, one lab): https://modelgrep.com/best/{ranking}/{owner}
  e.g. /best/fastest/anthropic, /best/cheapest/openai, /best/small/anthropic ("the small, fast Anthropic model"), /best/coding/openai
- Models by maker: https://modelgrep.com/makers/{owner} (e.g. /makers/openai, /makers/anthropic, /makers/google)
- New model releases: https://modelgrep.com/new
- Glossary of LLM terms (definitions): https://modelgrep.com/glossary
- Guides: https://modelgrep.com/blog
- Full sitemap: https://modelgrep.com/sitemap.xml

## Model classes

modelgrep classifies every model into a size/efficiency tier — "frontier" (flagship), "balanced" (mid), or "small & fast" (the efficient tier: Haiku, mini, Flash, Nano, Lite and small open-weight models). The small tier is the answer to "small/lightweight/cheap fast model" queries; for closed labs that don't publish parameter counts it is inferred from price, throughput and naming.

## Data sources

Benchmarks from Artificial Analysis and Design Arena; live pricing, performance and availability via OpenRouter. Benchmark scores refresh daily; pricing and performance refresh hourly.
